data.games#Games: Asteroids & Steroids Lasanha Frozen Blood In development Mushrun Neurohacker

data:
Play Asteroids And Steroids on itch.ioI made this game in 5 days to get some practice, after spending some time away from game development. I wanted to make a very simple game that I could finish quickly, so I decided to create a clone of the classic game Asteroids and add a few upgrades.
Music made by Matthew Pablo, inspired by Zigorous’s tutorial
The game is available for free on itch.io.

This may not seem like one of the most impressive models, but it has a pretty cool story behind it.
In 2022, Andrew Price, better known as Blender Guru, the biggest Blender tutorial channel on YouTube, created a collaborative campaign to raise money for the Blender Foundation, and as I really admire the free software initiative, and owe a lot of what I know today to Andrew, I decided to contribute.
The campaign asked each artist who wanted to take part to make a 3D doughnut, inspired by Andrew’s tutorials, which have become so popular that they are almost a trademark of those learning 3D modeling in Blender, to the point of being referenced in the wonderful film Everything Everywhere All at Once, winner of seven Oscars, including best picture.
Then he gathered the work of all 17,731 contributing artists into a single piece to be auctioned off, and selected the 30 best donuts to be auctioned off individually, and I was one of the chosen ones!
Press here to see the model

I made this game in 9 days for Game Jaaj 7, a game jam created by André Young, and the theme was archenemy.
This was the first Game Jam I ever took part in, and it was quite an experience.
It was 9 days in which I couldn’t think of anything else; all the free time I had was spent working on the game.
As it was my first game jam, I suffered from organizational problems, what’s expected when you’re starting out, the biggest of which was the famous scope creep, in my head my game was relatively simple, but for a solo dev, in just nine days, it was a bit much, so the enemies had serious collision problems, and the gameplay was very raw.
But apart from that, I’m very proud of this project, especially the visuals. I made all the models used in the game, and I think it turned out really well.
If you want to try out the game, it’s available for free on itch.io.
Frozen Blood by gabiru

data.postmortem#Postmortem:
Play Lasanha on itch.ioIdea After the controversies involving Unity, the engine I’ve been using to make my games since 2019, I decided to expand my horizons and, why not, have some fun?
I had plenty of options, there’s no shortage of powerful and accessible engines to learn, but I wanted something different, a challenge… so why not learn PICO-8?
I decided to make a game in honor of a frog I found inside a lasagna pan.
PICO-8 For those who don’t know, PICO-8 is a fictional retro console that imitates the graphic and sound limitations of 8-bit consoles like the SNES or Master System to encourage creativity without being intimidated by the countless possibilities of modern tools.
When you don’t have to worry about the smallest details, you can focus on what really matters: fun! It’s no wonder that one of the main uses of PICO-8 is prototyping - before Celeste won the award for best independent game of 2018, it was a simple PICO-8 game. (Another excellent tool for this is PuzzleScript)
So I decided to make a very simple game, inspired by Disc Room and Lasanha, the name I gave to that (huge) frog.
Postmortem I did all the programming, artwork and even music for the game! In the space of five days.
Despite the graphic and sound limitations of the engine, I think the game turned out cute. I also liked the music I made, even though it was my first time making music, I was very happy with the result.
The game is also available for free on itch.io.

data:
This game started as a silly joke between me and my girlfriend.
I asked her to choose random elements for me to put into a game.
She decided that the game would be about a mushroom that has to collect strawberries in a shopping mall.
That’s when Mu was born, my first original character, a little mushroom with eyeliner and a heart on her head.
I liked the idea so much that I couldn’t settle for something simple… I’ve tried to take this idea in different directions, but I think I’m finally getting there.
Beautiful artwork by my friend Clara.

My name is Gabriel de Carvalho, I make video games and talk about all kinds of things.
I’ve been making games and 3D art since 2019, and I write all kinds of things.
Art exists because life isn’t enough.
— Ferreira Gullar
Curriculum vitæ What does gabiru mean? It’s a nickname derived from my real name, Gabriel.
Furthermore, it’s a word with several meanings.
Gabiru is the clever one, the trickster. Gabiru is the cheerful one, the joker, the trickster. Gabiru is one of the devil’s names. Gabiru is the one who lives by the game (hehe). Where I was born, in the northeast of Brazil, that’s what they call the black rat.
What do you do? The areas I have the most practice/interest in are programming, tech art, 3D, level design, game design and writing.
But being a solo developer has made me learn a bit of everything, art, programming, design, UI/UX, music, etc. I have experience in almost everything, no matter how little. I love learning new things, I consider this to be my greatest asset as a professional.
Why do you make games? I make video games to express myself, I want to bring my ideas into the real world so that others can experience them, and I want to hear what you think about my creations, always evolving.
My art is inevitable, so why not make it my career?
What are your favorite games? Bloodborne - For rekindling my love of games. Zelda - For making me a kid again. Spelunky - For being the most fun game ever made. The Last of Us - For revolutionizing the narrative of games. Disco Elysium - For its creativity and incomparable courage. Garry’s Mod - For making me meet so many friends.
Essays Daydreams Games Movies Books Etc.
